Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / committed step toward isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate committed intermediate for target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and GmMAPK6-overexpressing hairy roots; downregulated in gmmyb4 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy root overexpression; leaf V3/R2 analyses; seed R8 analysis; transient Dual-LUC in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; overexpression lines; metabolite profiling; Y1H with MBW components; Dual-LUC reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIFS2 expression was significantly increased in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and reduced in gmmyb4 mutants. 2. Overexpression of GmIFS2 significantly increased TIC in R2-stage leaves and R8-stage seeds. 3. The GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 MBW complex bound the GmIFS2 promoter and repressed its reporter activity. 4. GmMYB4 alleviated this repression by disrupting MBW complex assembly. 5. GmMAPK6 overexpression also upregulated GmIFS2 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Regulated by GmMYB115; GmTT8; GmTTG1; indirectly by GmMYB4; responsive to GmMAPK6 module
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: GmIFS2pro::LUC reporter; native promoter tested in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq support for differential expression; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competes with anthocyanin branch for shared precursors
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Strong target for enhancing soybean isoflavone content through direct metabolic engineering or regulatory rewiring
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper specifically demonstrates its promoter is repressed by MBW complex and derepressed by GmMYB4-mediated complex disruption

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Anthocyanin branch competing with isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch for shared precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch enzyme; key competitor branch indicator
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Downregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and GmMAPK6-overexpressing hairy roots; upregulated in gmmyb4 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf analyses in transgenic lines; transient Dual-LUC in N. benthamiana; hairy root assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; anthocyanin quantification; Y1H with MBW components; Dual-LUC reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmANS3 showed an opposite expression pattern to isoflavone genes: reduced in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and increased in gmmyb4 mutants. 2. Anthocyanin content decreased in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and increased in gmmyb4 mutants, consistent with GmANS3 expression. 3. The GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 MBW complex bound the GmANS3 promoter and activated its reporter activity. 4. GmMYB4 reduced this activation by disrupting MBW complex formation. 5. GmMAPK6 overexpression also downregulated GmANS3.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Regulated by GmMYB115; GmTT8; GmTTG1; indirectly by GmMYB4; responsive to GmMAPK6 module
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin branch and opposes isoflavone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: GmANS3pro::LUC reporter; native promoter tested in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq support for differential expression; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation diverts common precursors away from isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful competing-branch target for rerouting flux toward isoflavones by reducing anthocyanin branch activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not an isoflavone enzyme, it is central to branch competition logic that determines final isoflavone accumulation

Regulation_Genes.Variety: Jack; NJAU_32 (cloning); GWAS panel of 219 soybean accessions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; contains bHLH TF-binding motif in R3 domain and EAR motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds; peak expression in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Three field environments in Nanjing (2014, 2015, 2018) for GWAS; greenhouse/growth chamber conditions; soybean hairy root transformation; leaf (R2/V3) and seed (R8) stages
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and branch selection between isoflavone and anthocyanin pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; overexpression; RNAi; CRISPR knockout; transcriptomics (RNA-seq); DAP-seq; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; Dual-LUC; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and haplotype analysis identified GmMYB4 as a favorable allele associated with higher glycitein class content. 2. Overexpression increased TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC in hairy roots, leaves and seeds, while RNAi/knockout reduced them. 3. GmMYB4 upregulated multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes including GmIFS2 and GmIFS1, but suppressed GmANS3 and anthocyanin accumulation. 4. GmMYB4 does not directly bind tested pathway gene promoters; instead it interacts with bHLH partners and disrupts the GmMYB115-GmTT8-TTG1 MBW complex. 5. This disruption relieves MBW-mediated repression of GmIFS2 and reduces activation of GmANS3, thereby shifting flux toward isoflavones. 6. GmMAPK6 physically interacts with and phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39 and also enhances its expression.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GmMAPK6-mediated signaling; phosphorylation by GmMAPK6 at Ser39
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional regulation by modulating MBW complex assembly; competitive binding to bHLH proteins; phosphorylation-associated activation/stimulation by upstream MAPK
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmIFS2; GmANS3; phenylpropanoid/isoflavone genes GmPAL1.3, GmC4H, GmCHS8, GmCHR1, GmCHI1, GmIFS1; MBW module components via interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/is associated with increased expression of GmPAL1.3, GmC4H, GmCHS8, GmCHR1, GmCHI1, GmIFS2 and GmIFS1; represses GmANS3 indirectly by disrupting MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator and branch-balance switch that redirects common precursors from anthocyanin toward isoflavone biosynthesis by disrupting MBW complexes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S:GmMYB4:GFP for localization; pMDC83 overexpression; pB7GWIWG2(2) RNAi; pBA002 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA); DAP-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA); GWAS using resequencing SNP dataset of 219 accessions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Anthocyanin content decreased by ~23% in overexpression leaves, indicating metabolic shift from anthocyanin branch
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Favorable haplotype GmMYB4H1 and transgene/editing target for improving soybean isoflavone content and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Double knockout with homolog Glyma.12g032200 caused stronger reductions, indicating functional compensation by homologous gene; also increased flavonoid and proanthocyanidin content in seeds

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression assays in N. benthamiana; yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoflavones benefit human health; anthocyanins are flavonoid pigments
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of competing flavonoid branches through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB115 functions as MYB component of an MBW complex with GmTT8 and GmTTG1. 2. GmMYB115 alone showed weak binding to promoters of multiple phenylpropanoid/isoflavone/anthocyanin genes, but binding was enhanced by GmTT8. 3. The MBW complex repressed GmIFS2 reporter activity and activated GmANS3 reporter activity. 4. GmMYB4 competes with GmMYB115 for GmTT8 binding, thereby disrupting this complex.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Competition from GmMYB4 for GmTT8 binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmANS3; GmOMT5; Gm4CL; GmIFS2; GmANR2; GmF3H1; GmUGT; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: With GmTT8/TTG1 complex, represses GmIFS2 and activates GmANS3; enhances binding to multiple pathway promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core MYB component of MBW complex that establishes opposite transcriptional outputs on isoflavone versus anthocyanin branch genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Via MBW complex, suppresses isoflavone branch gene expression and promotes anthocyanin branch gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely shifts flux toward anthocyanin branch at expense of isoflavones
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential regulatory target to rebalance flavonoid branch flux for isoflavone improvement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous study cited for GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 complex formation; this paper functionally tests the complex in target promoter/reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in N. benthamiana; yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of competing flavonoid branches as MBW component
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTT8 directly interacts with GmMYB4 and GmMYB115. 2. GmMYB4 competes with GmMYB115 for GmTT8 binding, disrupting the MBW complex. 3. In complex with GmMYB115, GmTT8 enhances promoter binding to multiple pathway genes. 4. The MBW complex containing GmTT8 represses GmIFS2 and activates GmANS3.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Competition by GmMYB4 for complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmANS3; GmOMT5; Gm4CL; GmIFS2; GmANR2; GmF3H1; GmUGT; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances GmMYB115 binding to promoters; in MBW complex represses GmIFS2 and activates GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central bHLH node whose partner choice determines whether MBW complex forms and how branch genes are regulated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports anthocyanin-promoting and isoflavone-repressing MBW activity unless displaced by GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S-driven constructs for interaction and reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Partitioning toward anthocyanin branch may reduce isoflavone accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for manipulating branch competition between isoflavones and anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG1 was not required for promoter binding discussion based on prior Arabidopsis evidence, but included in reporter assays as MBW component

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in N. benthamiana; yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Likely flavonoid regulatory network component via protein interaction with GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmGL3 physically interacts with GmMYB4 in LCI, BiFC and Y2H assays. 2. This supports that GmMYB4 can target multiple soybean bHLH partners in MBW-related regulation. 3. Direct downstream transcriptional effects of GmGL3 were not further dissected in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction within putative MBW-related regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive bHLH interactor providing mechanistic evidence that GmMYB4 associates with MBW-related bHLH factors
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mechanistically but not functionally resolved as major determinant in reporter or metabolite assays

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in N. benthamiana; yeast assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Likely flavonoid regulatory network component via protein interaction with GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmEGL3 physically interacts with GmMYB4 in LCI, BiFC and Y2H assays. 2. This indicates that GmMYB4 can engage multiple soybean bHLH proteins in MBW-related networks. 3. Its direct effects on isoflavone target genes were not further tested in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction within putative MBW-related regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive bHLH interactor providing mechanistic context for GmMYB4 action
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Currently limited; potential auxiliary regulatory target
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic interactor, but downstream metabolic causality not directly dissected here

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Dual-LUC assays in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MBW complex co-regulation of competing flavonoid branches
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-LUC (as MBW component); prior study cited for complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTTG1 was used as the WD-repeat component of the GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 MBW complex. 2. This MBW complex suppressed GmIFS2 promoter activity and activated GmANS3 promoter activity. 3. GmMYB4 addition reduced these MBW effects by disrupting complex formation through competition for GmTT8.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Complex assembly with GmMYB115 and GmTT8; indirectly affected by GmMYB4 competition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: As part of MBW complex, contributes to repression of GmIFS2 and activation of GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: MBW cofactor enabling branch-specific transcriptional regulation between isoflavone and anthocyanin pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports MBW-mediated decrease of isoflavone branch reporter and increase of anthocyanin branch reporter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Supports anthocyanin-promoting branch behavior at expense of isoflavone branch
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential complex component for branch-flux engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes TTG1 may not significantly affect promoter binding of TT2-TT8 complex based on Arabidopsis, but still includes it in functional reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soybean hairy root overexpression; transient assays in N. benthamiana; in vitro kinase assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots; inferred leaves/seeds via module
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal transduction controlling isoflavone biosynthesis and branch regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PPI prediction; BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H with constitutively active mutant; in vitro phosphorylation plus LC-MS/MS; hairy root overexpression; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPK6 physically interacts with GmMYB4. 2. Constitutively active GmMAPK6 phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39 in vitro. 3. Overexpression of GmMAPK6 in soybean hairy roots significantly increased isoflavone content. 4. GmMAPK6 overexpression upregulated GmMYB4 and key isoflavone pathway genes (GmPAL1.3, Gm4CL, GmCHI1, GmCHR1, GmIFS2, GmIFS1) and downregulated GmANS3. 5. GmMAPK6 is the upstream signaling component linking MAPK cascade to transcriptional control of isoflavone biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MAPK cascade signaling; upstream activator not experimentally resolved here
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation activation/modulation of GmMYB4 and positive transcriptional regulation of GmMYB4/pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmMYB4; GmPAL1.3; Gm4CL; GmCHI1; GmCHR1; GmIFS2; GmIFS1; GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates GmMYB4 and multiple isoflavone pathway genes; downregulates GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct for GmMYB4 phosphorylation; indirect for metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream MAPK switch connecting signaling to isoflavone accumulation through both transcriptional and post-translational control of GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Overexpression vector in hairy roots; HA-tag constructs; constitutively active CA-GmMAPK6D214G,E218A for kinase/Y2H assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: STRING PPI prediction; no accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential reduction of anthocyanin branch through GmANS3 downregulation; broader MAPK pleiotropy not assessed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering or breeding to enhance soybean isoflavones via signaling manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vivo phosphorylation validation remains future work according to discussion

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soybean hairy root overexpression; transient assays in N. benthamiana; Y2H with constitutively active mutant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal transduction associated with isoflavone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: STRING prediction; BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H with constitutively active mutant; hairy root overexpression; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPKK2 physically interacts with GmMYB4. 2. Overexpression in hairy roots increased isoflavone content. 3. However, compared with GmMAPK6, GmMAPKK2 had limited transcriptional effects, significantly affecting only Gm4CL, GmCHS8 and GmANS3. 4. Its role is supportive but less decisive than GmMAPK6 in the proposed signaling module.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MAPK cascade context; upstream regulators not resolved
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction/signaling kinase function; detailed phosphorylation output on GmMYB4 not demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression significantly affected Gm4CL and GmCHS8 positively and GmANS3 negatively
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Auxiliary MAPK-cascade component with positive but weaker effect on isoflavone accumulation than GmMAPK6
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases isoflavone content in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Overexpression vector in hairy roots; HA-tag construct; constitutively active CA-GmMAPKK2T220D,T226D for Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: STRING PPI prediction; no accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropy not assessed; weaker and partial transcriptional effects suggest less specific control
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Possible auxiliary engineering target, but lower priority than GmMAPK6/GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in interaction network and preliminary functional assays, but final model emphasizes GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW module rather than a definitive GmMAPKK2 role

Title: GmMYB4 Positively Regulates Isoflavone Biosynthesis via the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW Module in Soybean
